title: Past the river
summary: Yen has had three small bad things in one day. Bao takes her home a longer way — past the river — where they look and do not talk.

body.speaker: Yen
body.text: I do not know. The sky is grey. The bell was loud. My pencil broke.

body.speaker: Yen
body.text: Yes. But together they are big.

body.speaker: Yen
body.text: I walk home with you every day.

body.speaker: Bao
body.text: Today, we will walk a different way. Past the river.

body.speaker: Bao
body.text: Then we look at the river. We do not talk.

body.speaker: Bao
body.text: No. We look. For five minutes.

body.speaker: Bao
body.text: When I am sad, my father takes me to the river. He says the water carries the sadness.

body.speaker: Bao
body.text: Sometimes. Sometimes it does not. But you cannot be sad and look at the river at the same time.

body.speaker: Bao
body.text: Because the river is busy. It does not wait for you. If you are not looking, you miss it.

body.speaker: Bao
body.text: And Yen — I will buy you a new pencil tomorrow.

body.speaker: Bao
body.text: Because that is one of your three small things. I can fix one.

grammarNotes.title: 'Let's + verb' for a suggestion
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'Let's + động từ nguyên thể' để gợi ý làm điều gì đó cùng nhau. Trong bài: 'Okay. Let's go.' (Được. Mình đi nào.) 'Let's' là dạng rút gọn của 'Let us'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'Let's + base verb' to suggest doing something together. From the dialogue: 'Okay. Let's go.' 'Let's' is short for 'Let us'.

cloze.template:
Bao: Yen, are you {{b1}} today?
Yen: A little.
Bao: Why?
Yen: I do not know. The sky is {{b2}}. The bell was {{b3}}. My pencil {{b4}}.
Bao: Three small things.
Yen: Yes. But together they are big.
Bao: Walk home with me.
Yen: I walk home with you every day.
Bao: Today, we will walk a {{b5}} way. Past the {{b6}}.
Yen: It is {{b7}}.
Bao: Yes.
Yen: And then?
Bao: Then we look at the river. We do not talk.
Yen: We do not talk?
Bao: No. We look. For {{b8}} minutes.
Yen: Why?
Bao: When I am sad, my {{b9}} takes me to the river. He says the water {{b10}} the sadness.
Yen: Does it?
Bao: Sometimes. Sometimes it does not. But you cannot be sad and look at the river at the same time.
Yen: Why not?
Bao: Because the river is {{b11}}. It does not wait for you. If you are not looking, you miss it.
Yen: Okay. Let's go.
Bao: And Yen — I will buy you a new {{b12}} tomorrow.
Yen: Why?
Bao: Because that is one of your three small things. I can fix one.

blanks.explanation: Bao asks if Yen is sad — and she says 'a little'.
Yen lists 'the sky is grey' as one of her three small things.
The school bell was loud — the second small thing.
Her pencil broke — the third small thing.
Today they walk a different way — not the usual route.
Yen notes the way past the river is longer.
They will look for five minutes without talking.
Bao's father takes him to the river when he is sad.
The water 'carries the sadness' — takes it away.
The river is busy — always moving, full of motion.
Bao will buy Yen a new pencil — replacing the broken one.

translationVi:
Bảo: Yến, hôm nay cậu buồn à?
Yến: Một chút.
Bảo: Vì sao thế?
Yến: Tớ không biết. Trời xám. Chuông trường to. Bút chì của tớ gãy.
Bảo: Ba chuyện nhỏ.
Yến: Ừ. Nhưng gộp lại thì lớn.
Bảo: Đi về cùng tớ.
Yến: Ngày nào tớ chẳng đi về cùng cậu.
Bảo: Hôm nay mình đi đường khác. Đi qua sông.
Yến: Đường đó xa hơn mà.
Bảo: Đúng.
Yến: Rồi sao nữa?
Bảo: Rồi mình nhìn sông. Mình không nói.
Yến: Không nói à?
Bảo: Không. Mình nhìn. Trong năm phút.
Yến: Để làm gì?
Bảo: Khi tớ buồn, bố tớ dẫn tớ ra sông. Bố nói nước cuốn nỗi buồn đi.
Yến: Có thật vậy không?
Bảo: Đôi khi có. Đôi khi không. Nhưng cậu không thể vừa buồn vừa nhìn sông cùng lúc.
Yến: Sao không?
Bảo: Vì sông bận. Nó không chờ cậu. Nếu cậu không nhìn, cậu sẽ lỡ mất.
Yến: Được. Mình đi nào.
Bảo: Còn nữa — mai tớ mua cho cậu cây bút chì mới.
Yến: Sao thế?
Bảo: Vì đó là một trong ba chuyện nhỏ của cậu. Tớ sửa được một cái.

questions.explanation: Yen says she is 'a little' sad.

options: A test, a friend, a teacher
The grey sky, the loud bell, her broken pencil
Her bag, her shoes, her book

questions.prompt: What does Bao suggest they do?
options: Walk home a different way — past the river
questions.explanation: 'Today, we will walk a different way. Past the river.'

questions.prompt: What will they do when they reach the river?
options: Look at the river — without talking — for five minutes
questions.explanation: 'Then we look at the river. We do not talk.' / 'For five minutes.'
'When I am sad, my father takes me to the river.'

questions.prompt: What does Bao's father say the water does?
questions.explanation: 'He says the water carries the sadness.'

questions.prompt: Does Bao say the river always takes sadness away?
options: No — sometimes it does, sometimes it does not

questions.prompt: Why does Bao say you cannot be sad and look at the river at the same time?
options: Because the river is busy and does not wait — if you are not looking, you miss it
questions.explanation: 'The river is busy. It does not wait for you.'
questions.hint: Look at Bao's reply to 'Why not?'

questions.prompt: What does Bao promise Yen at the end?
questions.explanation: 'I will buy you a new pencil tomorrow.'

questions.prompt: Why does Bao buy the pencil?
options: Because the broken pencil was one of Yen's three small things — and Bao can fix one of them
questions.explanation: 'That is one of your three small things. I can fix one.'
questions.hint: Look at the very last line.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Bao says the river 'is busy. It does not wait for you. If you are not looking, you miss it.' Think of one small busy thing in your life — clouds, a flock of birds, traffic, the leaves. What changes if you look at it for five quiet minutes?
